entries.title: CATENA on james 4:7
entries.excerpt: If death came into the world by the malice of the devil, and Christ dwells in the inner man according to the Scriptures, this is the reason why he dwells in us, that he might destroy the death which has come upon us through the devil's cunning. And not only this, but that he might give us more grace as well. For he said: "I have come that they might have life, and have it more abundantly."

entries.title: SERMONS 77.7 on james 4:7
entries.excerpt: Let us fight as hard as we can, with the Lord's help, against that most harsh captivity of the soul [which is the devil's ability to divert our thoughts away from spiritual concerns].

entries.title: The Desert Fathers, Sayings of the Early Christian Monks on james 4:7
entries.excerpt: A brother came to Poemen and said to him, 'Many thoughts come into my mind and put me in danger.' He sent him out into the open air, and said, 'Open your lungs and do not breathe.' He replied, 'I can't do that.' Then he said to him: 'Just as you can't stop air coming into your lungs, so you can't stop thoughts coming into your mind. Your part is to resist them.'

entries.title: The Desert Fathers, Sayings of the Early Christian Monks on james 4:7
entries.excerpt: A brother felt hungry at dawn, and struggled not to eat till nine o'clock. When nine o'clock came, he made himself wait till noon. At noon he dipped his bread and sat down to eat, but then got up again, saying, 'I will wait till three.' At three o'clock he prayed, and saw the devil's work going out of him like smoke; and his hunger ceased.

entries.title: The Desert Fathers, Sayings of the Early Christian Monks on james 4:7
entries.excerpt: A hermit said, 'If anyone says "Forgive me", and humbles himself, he burns up the demons that tempt him.'
